content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

October 18, 2005

Adam Sanchez
Planning Commissioner
6675 San Rafael Road
Desert Hot Springs, CA 92240

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-05-202

Dear Mr. Sanchez:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

This letter is to notify you that we are withdrawing your advice request. It is the Commission's policy not to answer purely hypothetical questions, or questions where the facts presented are insufficient or too vague to render specific advice. (Regulation 18329(b)(2)(B), (8)(C) and (c)(4)(D), (F).)

In order for us to adequately advise you concerning your responsibilities under the campaign provisions of the Act, we would need to know whether you are a candidate, as well as whether the developer has a proceeding pending before you.

Since we do not have sufficient information to advise you with respect to your question, we have enclosed some materials that may be helpful to you.

_______________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. 05-202
Page No. 2

• Campaign Contributions May Cause Conflicts for Appointees and
Commissioners

• How Do I Get Advice From the FPPC?

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division
